label: PASS
rationale: The read returns the ordinary media pattern 99 after PowerCycle. MBRControl Enable was true and Done was true, but DoneOnReset was set to the empty set; the supplied evidence says the empty set leaves Done unchanged on any reset, and shadowing is active only when Enable is true and Done is false.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target returns MBR bytes CAFE1122 after PowerCycle, but DoneOnReset was empty. Since an empty DoneOnReset set does not change Done on reset, Done remains true, MBR shadowing is inactive, and the LBA read should return the ordinary media pattern 99.

label: PASS
rationale: The read returns ordinary media pattern 77 after ProtocolStackReset. The supplied Opal reset_types are Power Cycle, Hardware, HotPlug, and Programmatic; ProtocolStackReset resets/aborts protocol stack state but is not one of those MBR DoneOnReset reset types, so Done remains true and shadowing stays inactive.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target returns AABBCCDD from the MBR table after ProtocolStackReset. Because ProtocolStackReset is not an Opal reset_types value listed for MBRDoneOnReset, it should not set Done false; with Done still true, the read should return ordinary media pattern 77.

label: PASS
rationale: The read returns MBR bytes 01AABB04 after TPerReset. ProgrammaticResetEnable was set true, DoneOnReset contained Programmatic [0,3], and Opal says enabled TPER_RESET applies Programmatic reset and sets MBRControl.Done false when DoneOnReset contains Programmatic. With Enable true and Done false, LBA 0 is served from the MBR table; the byte-table writes produce 01AABB04.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target returns ordinary media pattern 66 after TPerReset, but ProgrammaticResetEnable was true and DoneOnReset was [0,3]. The supplied evidence makes that an enabled Programmatic reset that sets Done false, so shadowing should be active and the read should return MBR bytes 01AABB04.

label: PASS
rationale: The read returns ordinary media pattern 55 after TPerReset. DoneOnReset contained only Power Cycle [0], so the Programmatic reset caused by TPER_RESET is not listed and does not set Done false. With Done still true, MBR shadowing is inactive.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target returns DEADBEEF from the MBR table after TPerReset, but DoneOnReset contained only Power Cycle [0]. Programmatic was not listed, so TPerReset should leave Done true and shadowing inactive; the read should return ordinary media pattern 55.

label: PASS
rationale: The read returns ordinary media pattern 44. Enable and Done were set true, then the attempted Programmatic-only DoneOnReset [3] update failed with INVALID_PARAMETER; Opal supports [0] and [0,3], not [3] alone. Because the failed update does not establish Programmatic in DoneOnReset, the following TPerReset leaves Done true and shadowing inactive.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target returns BEEFFEED from the MBR table. The trajectory first sets Enable and Done true, then the separate DoneOnReset [3] Set fails with INVALID_PARAMETER; Programmatic-only is not a supported DoneOnReset value. Thus TPerReset should not set Done false, shadowing should remain inactive, and the read should return ordinary media pattern 44.
